questions.title: What is DIEM?
questions.markdown: **DIEM** is a tokenized compute unit equal to $1 per day of Venice API credit. It is an ERC-20 token minted from **sVVV** (staked VVV). Holding **DIEM** gives you predictable inference capacity on the Venice API or the ability to trade that capacity with others.

questions.title: What is the Contract Address for the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown:
The Venice token contract address is `0xacfE6019Ed1A7Dc6f7B508C02d1b04ec88cC21bf`.

This is the smart contract address for VVV on Base. You can view the contract, balances, and transaction history on [BaseScan](https://basescan.org/address/0xacfE6019Ed1A7Dc6f7B508C02d1b04ec88cC21bf).

questions.title: How much VVV has been burned?
questions.markdown: You can check the current burn of VVV here: [venice.ai/token/burns](https://venice.ai/token/burns).

questions.title: What happened to Points?
questions.markdown: The Points system has been retired. Points earned before December 31, 2024, were used to determine user VVV airdrop allocations.

questions.title: What are the tokenomics of the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown:
The VVV token has an initial supply of 100 million, allocated as follows: 50% to Venice users and the AI community via airdrop, 35% to [Venice.ai](https://venice.ai) for development and growth, 10% to an incentive fund to support ecosystem development, and 5% for liquidity provision. Annual emissions started at 10 million VVV per year at token launch, reduced to 8 million, then to 6 million in February 2026. You can view the current supply [here](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/venice-token/).

Fundamental demand comes from utility. You can stake VVV to earn yield and, when you want tokenized compute, lock your staked VVV (sVVV) to mint DIEM. Staked DIEM provides 1 dollar per day of Venice API credit per token, delivering private, uncensored inference without per-request fees. For example, staking 100 DIEM provides 100 dollars per day of API credit.

questions.title: Is the supply capped for the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown: Genesis supply was 100M VVV. On March 12, 2025, Venice executed two token burns totaling 33,539,739 VVV ([view transaction](https://basescan.org/tx/0xf5a19b79b13274faf19c46b404a36699990cad18e907aece0ab26671ff4a37af)). Annual emissions started at 10M VVV per year at token launch, reduced to 8M, then to 6M in February 2026. You can view the current supply [here](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/venice-token/).

questions.title: How does the Venice token (VVV) work and what are the benefits of staking it?
questions.markdown: You can stake VVV to earn emissions. You can also lock your staked VVV (sVVV) to mint **DIEM**, a tokenized compute unit equal to $1 per day of Venice API credit. You may stake **DIEM** for ongoing credit or send, sell, and transfer it, which lets you separate and monetize compute without selling your VVV principal. This expands VVV's utility from individual access to marketable capacity through **DIEM**.

questions.title: How does VVV staking yield work?
questions.markdown: After the **DIEM** upgrade the Utilization Rate split is removed. VVV stakers receive 100 percent of emissions as yield, paid in VVV. If your sVVV is locked to back minted **DIEM** you earn 80 percent of the standard staking yield while locked. Annual emissions are 10 million VVV effective August 20, 2025. At VVV launch emissions were 14 million per year and the Utilization Rate split emissions between stakers and Venice from 20 percent to 80 percent depending on demand.

questions.title: Does staking the Venice Token (VVV) grant access to Pro Features?
questions.markdown: Access to Pro Features is available to users who stake 100 or more Venice Tokens (VVV). Currently, users will still temporarily receive a staking yield on the staked 100 VVV. At a future date, this yield may be revoked, and Venice will give at least fifteen (15) days' notice. [Venice.ai](https://venice.ai) reserves the right to discontinue this staking benefit at any time, at its discretion.

questions.title: When are staking rewards paid out?
questions.markdown: Staking rewards accumulate continually and you can withdraw them whenever you wish.

questions.title: Do my staking rewards expire?
questions.markdown: No, staking rewards for $VVV do not expire. When claiming your rewards, you will need a small amount of $ETH on Base to cover gas fees.

questions.title: How long do my VVV tokens take to unstake?
questions.markdown: When you unstake your VVV, a **7-day cooldown period** begins. The specific amount you've chosen to unstake won't earn rewards during these 7 days. Any remaining VVV still actively staked will continue to accrue rewards normally. After the 7-day period, your unstaked VVV will be available for withdrawal.

questions.title: How do I stake VVV or DIEM?
questions.markdown:
Navigate to [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token) and connect your wallet in the top right. There you will see the options to stake VVV and DIEM.

**IMPORTANT: DO NOT SEND TOKENS DIRECTLY TO THE CONTRACT ADDRESS.**

Tokens sent to a contract address cannot be recovered. Always use the staking page.

questions.title: How do I mint DIEM?
questions.markdown: To mint **DIEM** you need to lock sVVV at the current Mint Rate. The Mint Rate determines how much sVVV is required to mint 1 **DIEM** and increases as supply grows. After minting, **DIEM** appears in your wallet as an ERC-20 token. You can mint on the Venice token dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: How do I use DIEM for inference?
questions.markdown:
Stake **DIEM** to receive $1 per day of API credit per token. This credit is perpetual and stable. You can stake **DIEM** on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

You'll see your **DIEM** allocation on the API page at [venice.ai/settings/api](https://venice.ai/settings/api).

questions.title: Is DIEM a stablecoin?
questions.markdown: No. **DIEM** is not pegged to USD. Its market price may move, but each **DIEM** always provides $1 per day of Venice API credit when staked.

questions.title: Can I transfer or trade DIEM?
questions.markdown: Yes. **DIEM** is an ERC-20 token on Base. You can send it to other wallets and trade it on supported platforms.

questions.title: Is there a cooldown to unstake DIEM?
questions.markdown: DIEM can be unstaked after a 1 day cooldown. To unlock sVVV, burn the same amount of DIEM you minted. Any unlocked sVVV can then be unstaked with a 7 day cooldown.

questions.title: How do I unlock my sVVV after minting DIEM?
questions.markdown: Burn the same amount of **DIEM** you minted to unlock **sVVV**. Partial burns are allowed. Once unlocked, sVVV can be unstaked with a 7 day cooldown. You can burn on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: What happens if I sell the DIEM I minted and later want to unlock my sVVV?
questions.markdown: To unlock sVVV you must burn the same amount of **DIEM** you minted. If you sold that **DIEM** you will need to re-acquire it before you can unlock. While locked, your VVV continues to earn yield.

questions.title: If part of my sVVV is in the 7-day unstake cooldown, can I still mint DIEM with the rest?
questions.markdown: Yes. The cooldown applies only to the specific amount you chose to unstake. Any remaining sVVV stays staked and can be locked to mint DIEM at the current Mint Rate. You can mint on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: Does burning DIEM give me more sVVV?
questions.markdown:
No. Burning `DIEM` only unlocks the `sVVV` that was originally locked when that `DIEM` was minted.

If you buy `DIEM` on the market and burn it, that does **not** create new `sVVV` or add `sVVV` to your balance. Burning only unlocks the already-locked `sVVV` tied to **your original minting position**.

questions.title: Why do my unclaimed rewards disappear when I stake VVV?
questions.markdown: Your rewards aren't lost - staking additional tokens automatically claims your pending rewards. You can verify this in your transaction history on [BaseScan](https://basescan.org/).

questions.title: What yield do I earn while my sVVV is locked to back DIEM?
questions.markdown: While your sVVV is locked to back **DIEM** you earn 80 percent of the standard VVV staking yield. VVV staked without minting **DIEM** earns 100 percent of emissions. Before the **DIEM** upgrade, VVV stakers earned between 20 percent and 80 percent of emissions.

questions.title: What’s the difference between web search, web scraping, and X Search?
questions.markdown:
These features bring fresh information into Venice in different ways.

- `Web Search` finds current results from the web and helps Venice answer questions with up-to-date information.
- `Web Scraping` pulls content from specific URLs and adds that content to context.
- `X Search` is available on supported Grok models and can search both the web and X in real time.

Use `Web Search` when you want general current information. Use `Web Scraping` when you want Venice to read a specific page. Use `X Search` when you want supported Grok models to use xAI’s native real-time search.

For more details, see the [Web Search docs](https://docs.venice.ai/api-reference/endpoint/augment/search) and [API Pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing).

questions.title: What are the web scraping limits in the Venice UI?
questions.markdown:
When Web Scraping is enabled and your prompt includes URLs, Venice applies a daily scrape limit based on your plan:

- `Anonymous`: 0 per day
- `FREE`: 15 per day
- `PRO`: 100 per day
- `PLUS`: 400 per day
- `MAX`: 1,000 per day

questions.title: How do I generate the same person/face/subject in an image?
questions.markdown:
You can create a consistent person, face, or subject in an image by using image editing or seed values.

One option is to use image editing to modify an existing image while keeping the same subject. Another option is to use seed values so you can generate variations while preserving a similar look.

To use seed values: 1. Create a detailed prompt describing the subject you want. 2. Generate your image. 3. When you find an image you like, open `Image Details`. 4. Copy the seed value. 5. Enter that seed in your image settings. 6. Adjust your prompt while keeping the same seed to create variations of the same subject.

questions.title: How do I make a video from a reference image?
questions.markdown:
To make a video from a reference image, upload the image, choose a video model that supports reference-to-video, and describe the motion, scene, or transformation you want.

This is useful when you want the video to keep a consistent subject, character, or visual identity.

Video generations consume credits. For current model availability and video details, see the [video model docs](https://docs.venice.ai/models/video).

questions.title: Can I upload images into Venice?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Venice supports image uploads for chat, image editing, and other multimodal use cases.

Not all models support vision or image analysis. If you upload an image for analysis while using a model that does not support vision, Venice will swap to a model that does.

You can upload an image when you want Venice to analyze it, use it as the basis for an edit, or use it in a generation workflow.

questions.title: How do I disable the Mature Filter?
questions.markdown:
You can disable the Mature Filter if you have a `Pro` plan or higher.

Open `Settings` and turn the Mature Filter off. You can also access this setting from the model selector.

questions.title: How do I remove the Venice watermark on images?
questions.markdown:
You can remove the Venice watermark on images if you have a `Pro` plan or higher.

Open `Settings`, go to image settings, and turn the watermark option off. You can also access this setting from the prompt bar while using an image model.

questions.title: How do I upload a file for analysis?
questions.markdown:
To upload a file for analysis, use the `+` button to open the action menu and attach your file.

Once the file is uploaded, enter your prompt telling Venice what you want to do with it, such as summarize it, answer questions about it, or analyze specific sections.

All models support document upload.

questions.title: How large of a document can Venice analyze?
questions.markdown:
Venice supports documents up to `10MB` and approximately `250,000` characters.

If you receive an error after uploading a document, the file likely exceeds the current size or length limit.

questions.title: How do I edit an image on Venice?
questions.markdown:
You can edit an image on Venice in three ways.

The first is to use the `+` button to open the action menu and select `Edit Image`, then upload the image you want to change and describe the edit you want in plain language.

You can also edit an image while in `Auto` mode by attaching an image with the `+` button and prompting Venice to make the change you want.

A third option is to use `Venice Studio`, available in the left sidebar, where you can work with supported image editing workflows directly.

Image editing can be used to replace objects, change backgrounds, adjust style, or make more targeted changes to an existing image. Image edits consume credits or image requests depending on the model and workflow you are using.

questions.title: How do I access the API?
questions.markdown:
You can access the Venice API in two ways.

The standard way is to create an API key in your [API Dashboard](https://venice.ai/settings/api). Once you have an API key, you can use the API with your available `USD credits` or `DIEM`.

Venice also supports [\`x402\`](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api), which is an alternative way to access paid API routes using wallet authentication instead of an API key.

For setup details and pricing, see the [API pricing docs](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing) and the [\`x402\` guide](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api).

questions.title: Where can I see my API usage and credit balances?
questions.markdown:
You can view your API usage, credit spend, and balance details in your [API Dashboard](https://venice.ai/settings/api).

This page shows your API activity and separates your available balance by `DIEM` and `USD`, so you can see how your credits are split.

If you’re trying to understand where your API credits went, this is the best place to check first.

questions.title: Where can I find the API documentation?
questions.markdown:
You can find the Venice API documentation at [docs.venice.ai](https://docs.venice.ai).

For API setup, endpoints, pricing, and rate limits, start with the [API overview](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/about-venice).

questions.title: Is API usage included in my Pro, Pro+, or Max plan?
questions.markdown:
No. Plan perks like unlimited text prompts and daily image limits refer to use inside the Venice web app and mobile app, not the API.

API usage is metered separately. Every API call consumes credits (or charges your balance) based on the model's per-token price, regardless of whether you're on Pro, Pro+, or Max. Paid plans do not include unlimited API usage.

If you use a text model via the API and see your balance going down, that is expected. Even for models that are free inside the web app, API calls are billed per token.

You can find the per-model API rates at [docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing#api-pricing).

questions.title: How do I install Venice on my phone or tablet?
questions.markdown:
Venice is currently available on `Android`.

You can download the app here: [venice.ai/lp/download](https://venice.ai/lp/download). From there, you can install via Google Play or download the Android APK directly.

The **iOS app is temporarily unavailable on the App Store**. We're working to bring it back as soon as possible. If you're on iPhone, you can keep using Venice in the meantime at [venice.ai](https://venice.ai) right from your mobile browser.

questions.title: Can I export an encrypted backup on my mobile device?
questions.markdown:
No. Encrypted Backups are currently managed from the Venice web app, not the iOS or Android mobile apps.

On the web app, Pro users can create an encrypted backup from the chat history drawer by selecting their profile and choosing `Backup Chat History`.

If you need to back up or restore chat history, use Venice in a web browser on a logged-in device.

questions.title: Is there a limit to how much I can use Venice?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Your limits depend on your plan and the feature you’re using.

Free includes 10 text prompts per day and 15 image prompts per day. Pro includes unlimited text prompts, 1,000 image prompts per day, and 100 monthly credits for premium features. Pro+ includes higher image limits plus 7,500 monthly credits, and Max includes the highest image limits plus 22,500 monthly credits.

Premium features such as video, music, frontier models, and API usage consume credits. For the latest limits and plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: What are Venice Credits?
questions.markdown:
Venice Credits are used to access premium features on the platform, including video generation, music, premium models, and API usage.

Every paid plan includes monthly credits. Pro includes 100 per month, Pro+ includes 7,500 per month, and Max includes 22,500 per month. Pro+ includes 2-month credit banking, and Max includes 3-month credit banking.

For the latest plan details and included credits, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing) and [Introducing Venice Pro+ and Max](https://venice.ai/blog/new-subscription-tiers).

questions.title: When do Venice credits reset?
questions.markdown:
Venice credits do not all reset on the same schedule.

Paid plan credits refresh monthly. Pro includes 100 credits per month, Pro+ includes 7,500 per month, and Max includes 22,500 per month.

DIEM-backed credits are different. Credits from staked DIEM refresh daily at `12:01 AM UTC`.

USD credits are also different from DIEM-backed credits. USD credits do not reset daily, and they are not the same as DIEM-backed daily credits.

If you are checking your balance, make sure you know which kind of credits you are using: monthly plan credits, DIEM-backed daily credits, or USD credits. For current plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: Is there a limit to Voice Mode usage?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Voice Mode in the Venice web and mobile apps is limited by a rolling quota of voice audio seconds per 30-day period. The allowance depends on your plan.

- **Pro: **`3,600 seconds` (1 hour per 30 days)
- **Pro+: **`18,000 seconds` (5 hours per 30 days)
- **Max: **`72,000 seconds` (20 hours per 30 days)

These limits apply to Voice Mode usage in the app; they are separate from unlimited text prompts and other plan allowances.

questions.title: What is the refund policy?
questions.markdown:
Venice subscriptions are generally non-refundable once payment has been processed. If you do not want your subscription to renew, you can cancel it before the next billing cycle and your access will remain active through the end of your current paid period.

To manage or cancel your subscription, visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription) or see [How do I cancel my subscription?](https://venice.ai/faqs#how-do-i-cancel-my-subscription).

questions.title: How does the referral program work?
questions.markdown:
When someone signs up using your referral link and upgrades to `Pro`, both of you receive `$10` in API credits.

A referral only qualifies after the referred user completes the required upgrade. You can track referrals from the referral section in Venice.

If you want more details about current plans and credits, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: Why didn’t I get my referral credits?
questions.markdown:
Referral credits are only issued after the referred user completes a qualifying `Pro` upgrade.

If your credits have not appeared yet, check that: - the new user signed up through your referral link - they completed the qualifying upgrade - the referral appears in your referral dashboard - the payment was not refunded or reversed

If everything looks correct and the credits still have not appeared, contact [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

questions.title: Can I pay with crypto?
questions.markdown:
Venice does not broadly accept crypto for all purchases.

Credits can be purchased with `USD` or `USDC`. For subscriptions, `BTC` is accepted through `BTCPay` for yearly plans.

Because payment options depend on what you are buying, always use the payment methods shown in the relevant checkout or billing flow. For current plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: What are Pro+ and Max?
questions.markdown:
`Pro+` and `Max` are higher-tier Venice plans for users who need more credits and higher usage limits than `Pro`.

`Pro+` includes everything in `Pro`, plus higher image generation limits, `7,500` monthly credits, and `2-month` credit banking. `Max` includes everything in `Pro+`, plus the highest image generation limits, `22,500` monthly credits, and `3-month` credit banking.

If you need more credits for video, music, premium models, or API usage, these plans are designed for heavier use. For the latest plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing) and [Introducing Venice Pro+ and Max](https://venice.ai/blog/new-subscription-tiers).

questions.title: How do I cancel my subscription?
questions.markdown:
You can cancel your subscription at any time from your subscription settings.

To manage or cancel your subscription, visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription). If you cancel, your access will remain active until the end of your current billing period.

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription, payment methods, or get receipts?
questions.markdown:
You can manage your subscription, payment methods, billing details, and receipts from your subscription settings.

Visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription) to view or update your billing information.

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription if I purchased through Apple?
questions.markdown:
If you subscribed through the Apple App Store, your subscription is managed directly by Apple.

To manage or cancel it, open the `Settings` app on your iPhone or iPad, tap your name, select `Subscriptions`, and choose `Venice.ai`.

You can also manage Apple subscriptions at [apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions](https://apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions).

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription if I purchased through Google Play?
questions.markdown:
If you subscribed through Google Play, your subscription is managed directly by Google.

To manage or cancel it, open the Google Play Store, tap your profile icon, select `Payments & subscriptions`, then `Subscriptions`, and choose `Venice.ai`.

You can also manage Google Play subscriptions at [play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions](https://play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions).

questions.title: Why have I hit my image limit early?
questions.markdown:
Features like `Enhance`, `Upscale`, and `Multi-Image Edit` use more image requests per use than a standard image generation.

For example: - `Enhance 4x` can use up to 16 image requests - `Multi-Image Edit` uses 4 image requests instead of 2

These image request limits apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How does the Enhance feature consume image requests?
questions.markdown:
The `Enhance` feature uses image requests based on the output size. `Enhance 4x` can use up to 16 image requests per use.

These image request rules apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How do Venice image requests work?
questions.markdown:
Venice gives you a set number of image requests for `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Different actions use those requests at different rates.

- `Generating a single image`: 1 request
- `Editing an image`: 2 requests
- `Multi-image editing`: 4 requests
- `Generating 2, 3, or 4 image variants`: 1 request per image
- `2x Upscale (with enhance)`: up to 8 requests
- `4x Upscale (with enhance)`: up to 16 requests
- `2x Upscale (without enhance)`: up to 10 requests
- `4x Upscale (without enhance)`: up to 20 requests

You can mix and match these actions until all the image requests included in your subscription plan are used up. These limits reset based on your first request in each rate-limit period.

Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How does the Edit Image feature consume image requests?
questions.markdown:
The `Edit Image` feature uses 2 image requests per edit, regardless of the size or complexity of the edit.

`Multi-image editing` uses 4 image requests per operation.

These image request rules apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: Where can I find how many images I have left for the day?
questions.markdown:
To see how many image requests you have left, open the chat history drawer, go to `Settings`, then open `Storage and Limits`.

Your remaining text and image limits are shown there.

These limits apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: Is API usage included in my Pro, Pro+, or Max plan?
questions.markdown:
No. Plan perks like unlimited text prompts and daily image limits refer to use inside the Venice web app and mobile app, not the API.

API usage is metered separately. Every API call consumes credits (or charges your balance) based on the model's per-token price, regardless of whether you're on Pro, Pro+, or Max. Paid plans do not include unlimited API usage.

If you use a text model via the API and see your balance going down, that is expected. Even for models that are free inside the web app, API calls are billed per token.

You can find the per-model API rates at [docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing#api-pricing).

questions.title: How do I delete my account?
questions.markdown:
You can delete your account from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and choose `Delete Account`.

Once an account is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

questions.title: How do I change my account password?
questions.markdown:
You can change your password from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and choose `Update Password`.

questions.title: How do I change my account email?
questions.markdown:
To change your account email, first add the new email address to your account, then set it as your primary email.

You can do this from your account settings by opening `Settings`, going to `Profile`, selecting `Manage Account`, and updating your email settings there.

questions.title: How do I add 2FA on my account?
questions.markdown:
You can enable two-factor authentication from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and enable two-factor authentication (`2FA`) for your account.

questions.title: What is the official support email?
questions.markdown: The official email for support is [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

questions.title: Where should I report a security vulnerability?
questions.markdown: Please submit security vulnerabilities through the [Venice Bug Bounty Program](/bug-bounty). The program page explains what is in scope, what to include in your report, and how rewards are handled.

questions.title: WalletConnect users: Why do I keep getting logged out of Venice?
questions.markdown:
WalletConnect sessions expire after 7 days, so if you sign in to Venice with WalletConnect, you may need to log in again after that period.

If your wallet supports longer sessions, you may be able to adjust that in your wallet settings. For wallet-specific session questions, contact your wallet provider.

questions.title: Can you delete my data?
questions.markdown:
You can request deletion of the account information Venice stores, such as your email address.

To submit a deletion request, contact [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

For more information about Venice’s privacy approach, see [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy).

questions.title: How do I join the official Venice.ai Discord?
questions.markdown:
You can join the official Venice Discord here: [discord.gg/askvenice](https://discord.gg/askvenice).

On Discord, you can get announcements from the team, connect with the community, and follow product updates.

featured.title: How does Web Search work on Venice?
featured.markdown:
Web Search lets Venice bring in current information from the web when you need fresher results than a model’s training data alone can provide.

When Web Search is enabled, Venice can use supported search providers to retrieve up-to-date results and use them in its response. Venice supports privacy-preserving search options, including Brave with zero data retention and anonymized Google search.

If you need current information, make sure Web Search is enabled before you ask your question. For more details, see the [Web Search docs](https://docs.venice.ai/api-reference/endpoint/augment/search).

featured.title: What is the difference between the AI models on Venice?
featured.markdown:
Venice offers a range of models with different strengths, capabilities, and privacy modes.

Some models are better for writing, coding, reasoning, or long-context work. Others are optimized for image, audio, video, or other specialized tasks. Models also differ in privacy mode, including `Anonymized`, `Private`, `TEE`, and `E2EE`, so you can choose the level of privacy that fits your use case.

There is no single best model for everything. The best choice depends on what you want to do, how much context you need, and what privacy guarantees you want. You can learn more about Venice’s privacy modes in [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai).

featured.title: What are the context lengths for Venice models?
featured.markdown:
Context length depends on the model you choose, and it can change as models are added, updated, or deprecated.

Some Venice models support much larger context windows than others, so the best place to check the current limit is the model details shown in Venice or the live model documentation. You can also view context window details in the model selector in the prompt bar by hovering over a model.

featured.title: How does Venice handle my privacy and data?
featured.markdown:
Venice is designed around privacy by default.

Your conversations are stored locally on your device, and Venice does not store or log your prompts or model responses on its servers. Venice also offers multiple privacy modes so you can choose how your data is handled when using different models.

These privacy modes include `Anonymized`, `Private`, `TEE`, and `E2EE`. To learn more, see [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy) and [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai).

featured.title: How does memory work on Venice?
featured.markdown:
Venice uses a privacy-preserving memory system that stores memories locally in your browser instead of on Venice servers.

Memory helps Venice remember useful context from past conversations, such as preferences, recurring topics, and important details you want it to retain. When memory is enabled, Venice can use that stored context to make future conversations more helpful and personalized.

You can manage memory from `Settings > Memory`. To learn more, see [Memoria: A Technical Overview of Venice's Memory System](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-memoria-technical-overview).

featured.title: How do I switch models in Venice?
featured.markdown:
To switch models, click the current model name in the prompt bar to open the model selector.

From there, you can browse or search for the model you want and switch at any time. The model selector also shows model details so you can compare them before choosing one.

featured.title: How do I upload a file for analysis?
featured.markdown:
To upload a file for analysis, use the `+` button to open the action menu and attach your file.

Once the file is uploaded, enter your prompt telling Venice what you want to do with it, such as summarize it, answer questions about it, or analyze specific sections.

All models support document upload.

featured.title: How do I access the API?
featured.markdown:
You can access the Venice API in two ways.

The standard way is to create an API key in your [API Dashboard](https://venice.ai/settings/api). Once you have an API key, you can use the API with your available `USD credits` or `DIEM`.

Venice also supports [\`x402\`](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api), which is an alternative way to access paid API routes using wallet authentication instead of an API key.

For setup details and pricing, see the [API pricing docs](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing) and the [\`x402\` guide](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api).

featured.title: How do I generate the same person/face/subject in an image?
featured.markdown:
You can create a consistent person, face, or subject in an image by using image editing or seed values.

One option is to use image editing to modify an existing image while keeping the same subject. Another option is to use seed values so you can generate variations while preserving a similar look.

To use seed values: 1. Create a detailed prompt describing the subject you want. 2. Generate your image. 3. When you find an image you like, open `Image Details`. 4. Copy the seed value. 5. Enter that seed in your image settings. 6. Adjust your prompt while keeping the same seed to create variations of the same subject.

questions.title: What is the difference between the AI models on Venice?
questions.markdown:
Venice offers a range of models with different strengths, capabilities, and privacy modes.

Some models are better for writing, coding, reasoning, or long-context work. Others are optimized for image, audio, video, or other specialized tasks. Models also differ in privacy mode, including `Anonymized`, `Private`, `TEE`, and `E2EE`, so you can choose the level of privacy that fits your use case.

There is no single best model for everything. The best choice depends on what you want to do, how much context you need, and what privacy guarantees you want. You can learn more about Venice’s privacy modes in [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai).

questions.title: What are the context lengths for Venice models?
questions.markdown:
Context length depends on the model you choose, and it can change as models are added, updated, or deprecated.

Some Venice models support much larger context windows than others, so the best place to check the current limit is the model details shown in Venice or the live model documentation. You can also view context window details in the model selector in the prompt bar by hovering over a model.

If you plan to work with long prompts, long chats, or large files, check the model’s current context window before you start. See the current model docs at [Models](https://docs.venice.ai/models/overview) and [API Pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing).

questions.title: How is Venice different from ChatGPT, Claude, or other AI services?
questions.markdown:
Venice is built around privacy, model choice, and user control.

Venice gives users access to a wide range of models and privacy modes, including `Private`, `TEE`, and `E2EE`, so you can choose how your data is handled. Venice is also designed around neutral model access, while still making it clear that individual models may have their own built-in limitations or policies.

If privacy, control, and flexible model choice matter to you, that is where Venice stands out most. You can read more at [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy) and in the [privacy docs](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/privacy).

questions.title: What do the privacy badges on Venice models mean?
questions.markdown:
Every model on Venice shows a privacy mode so you can see how your data is handled before you use it.

- `Anonymized`: Venice hides your identity from the model provider, but the provider may still process your prompts under its own policies.
- `Private`: Your prompts are processed on Venice-controlled or zero-data-retention partner infrastructure and are not retained.
- `TEE`: Your prompts are processed inside a Trusted Execution Environment, where privacy is enforced by secure hardware.
- `E2EE`: Your prompt is encrypted on your device, stays encrypted in transit, and is only decrypted inside a verified secure enclave.

If you want the strongest privacy guarantees, choose a model with `E2EE`. To learn more about the four privacy modes, see [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai) and [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy).

questions.title: What’s the difference between TEE and E2EE on Venice?
questions.markdown:
Both `TEE` and `E2EE` offer stronger privacy than standard inference, but they protect your data in different ways.

`TEE` means your prompt is processed inside a secure hardware enclave, so privacy is enforced by hardware rather than policy alone. `E2EE` goes further by encrypting your prompt on your device before it leaves, so it stays encrypted until it is decrypted inside the secure enclave.

Because `E2EE` keeps your prompt encrypted end to end, it has more limitations than `TEE`. Features like web search and memory are disabled in `E2EE` mode because they would require your prompt to be decrypted outside the enclave.

If you want the strongest privacy option, use `E2EE`. If you want hardware-verified privacy with broader feature support, use `TEE`. For more details, see [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai).

questions.title: How does Web Search work on Venice?
questions.markdown:
Web Search lets Venice bring in current information from the web when you need fresher results than a model’s training data alone can provide.

When Web Search is enabled, Venice can use supported search providers to retrieve up-to-date results and use them in its response. Venice supports privacy-preserving search options, including Brave with zero data retention and anonymized Google search.

If you need current information, make sure Web Search is enabled before you ask your question. For more details, see the [Web Search docs](https://docs.venice.ai/api-reference/endpoint/augment/search).

questions.title: How can I manage my conversation history and start new chats?
questions.markdown:
You can start a new chat at any time when you want a fresh context or want to keep topics separate.

Your previous conversations are available in the chat history drawer on the left sidebar, so you can return to earlier chats when needed. Starting a new chat does not delete your older conversations. It simply starts a separate thread.

If you want clearer organization or a cleaner context for a new task, starting a new chat is the best option.

questions.title: What happens if I switch models in the middle of my conversation?
questions.markdown:
You can switch models within the same conversation without starting over.

Changing models does not reset the conversation. Your chat will continue until you choose to start a new one.

questions.title: Can I access my chat history across different browsers or devices?
questions.markdown:
Venice prioritizes user privacy by ensuring that all conversation data remains solely on your device. This means that your chat history is stored locally within your browser and is not uploaded or saved on Venice's servers. As a result, when you access Venice from a different device or browser, your previous conversations won't be available because they were never stored centrally. This approach guarantees that your interactions are private.

There are a few ways to work around this, the first is by sharing conversations across devices using encrypted links ([Can I share my text chat conversation?](https://venice.ai/faqs#can-i-share-my-text-chat-conversation)). And the second is by using encrypted backups: [How do Encrypted Backups on Venice Work?](https://venice.ai/faqs#how-do-encrypted-backups-on-venice-work)

questions.title: How do I prevent my chat history from being cleared by my browser?
questions.markdown:
We've added a feature to help minimize data loss: Chat Persistence.

To enable Chat Persistence:

1. Open the **Chat History Drawer** in the top left.
2. Select **Settings**.
3. Click **Storage and Limits**.
4. Turn on **Persist Storage**.

questions.title: Can I share my text chat conversation?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Venice lets you create an encrypted share link for text-based chats.

The share button appears under the last response from the LLM in a chat. When you click it, Venice creates a shareable encrypted link to that text conversation up to that point.

Anyone with the link can view the conversation up to the point where the link was created, and they can continue from there in their own browser. Shared text conversation links are available for a limited time.

Sharing is only supported for text-based chats.

questions.title: How does Venice handle my privacy and data?
questions.markdown:
Venice is designed around privacy by default.

Your conversations are stored locally on your device, and Venice does not store or log your prompts or model responses on its servers. Venice also offers multiple privacy modes so you can choose how your data is handled when using different models.

These privacy modes include `Anonymized`, `Private`, `TEE`, and `E2EE`. To learn more, see [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy) and [Venice Launches End-to-End Encrypted AI](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-launches-end-to-end-encrypted-ai).

questions.title: How do Encrypted Backups on Venice Work?
questions.markdown:
Encrypted Backups are a Pro feature currently managed from the Venice web app, not the iOS or Android mobile apps. To create one, access the chat history drawer, select your profile, and select `Backup Chat History`.

Here’s how they work:

1. On your local device, Venice encrypts your data with a password that only you control.
2. The encrypted backup is broken into chunks and uploaded to Venice’s infrastructure.
3. You can later download and restore that backup on another logged-in web device, either by overwriting your existing history or merging it.

A few important notes: - Venice does not have any record of the password you create, so if you lose it, your backup cannot be recovered. - You are limited to a maximum of 5 concurrent backups. - Backups expire after 90 days.

questions.title: How does memory work on Venice?
questions.markdown:
Venice uses a privacy-preserving memory system that stores memories locally in your browser instead of on Venice servers.

Memory helps Venice remember useful context from past conversations, such as preferences, recurring topics, and important details you want it to retain. When memory is enabled, Venice can use that stored context to make future conversations more helpful and personalized.

You can manage memory from `Settings > Memory`. To learn more, see [Memoria: A Technical Overview of Venice's Memory System](https://venice.ai/blog/venice-memoria-technical-overview).

questions.title: What languages are supported?
questions.markdown:
Venice supports multiple languages, but language support depends on the model you are using.

To check a model’s language capabilities, open the model selector and view that model’s details. The Venice interface is also available in multiple languages.

questions.title: How do I enable Voice Mode?
questions.markdown:
To enable Voice Mode, open the settings menu from the prompt bar while using a text model.

From there, go to the voice settings and turn on `Read Responses`. You can also choose the language, voice, and playback speed there.

questions.title: How do I switch models in Venice?
questions.markdown:
To switch models, click the current model name in the prompt bar to open the model selector.

From there, you can browse or search for the model you want and switch at any time. The model selector also shows model details so you can compare them before choosing one.

questions.title: How do I switch between text and image?
questions.markdown:
To switch between text and image generation, click the current model name in the prompt bar to open the model selector.

From there, choose a text model to chat or an image model to generate images. You can switch at any time without losing your existing conversation.

questions.title: How do I switch to Dark Mode?
questions.markdown:
You can switch to Dark Mode from your settings.

Open `Settings`, go to the `Theme` section, and choose `Dark` under the color scheme options.

questions.title: How do I add line breaks or start a new paragraph in my messages?
questions.markdown:
You can change how the Enter key behaves in your app settings.

Open the settings menu from the prompt bar, go to the app settings, and turn off `Enter Submits Chat`. After that, pressing Enter will add a new line instead of sending your message.

questions.title: How do I import memories from another AI assistant?
questions.markdown:
To import memories from another AI assistant, open `Settings`, go to `Memory`, and find `Import Memories`.

From there, follow the steps to generate and import a memory summary from another service. If you already have a memory summary prepared, you can paste it there and complete the import.

questions.title: What is the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown: VVV is the foundational asset of Venice, built to power a programmable AI economy. You can stake VVV to earn yield, and you can lock your staked VVV (sVVV) to mint DIEM, then stake DIEM for $1 per day of API credit per token. DIEM makes AI compute ownable, tradeable, and transferable, so capacity can move between agents, bots, and applications or be monetized without selling your VVV. VVV is the foundation of the Venice market, and DIEM is its unit of compute.

questions.title: What is DIEM?
questions.markdown: **DIEM** is a tokenized compute unit equal to $1 per day of Venice API credit. It is an ERC-20 token minted from **sVVV** (staked VVV). Holding **DIEM** gives you predictable inference capacity on the Venice API or the ability to trade that capacity with others.

questions.title: What is the Contract Address for the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown:
The Venice token contract address is `0xacfE6019Ed1A7Dc6f7B508C02d1b04ec88cC21bf`.

This is the smart contract address for VVV on Base. You can view the contract, balances, and transaction history on [BaseScan](https://basescan.org/address/0xacfE6019Ed1A7Dc6f7B508C02d1b04ec88cC21bf).

questions.title: How much VVV has been burned?
questions.markdown: You can check the current burn of VVV here: [venice.ai/token/burns](https://venice.ai/token/burns).

questions.title: What happened to Points?
questions.markdown: The Points system has been retired. Points earned before December 31, 2024, were used to determine user VVV airdrop allocations.

questions.title: What are the tokenomics of the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown:
The VVV token has an initial supply of 100 million, allocated as follows: 50% to Venice users and the AI community via airdrop, 35% to [Venice.ai](https://venice.ai) for development and growth, 10% to an incentive fund to support ecosystem development, and 5% for liquidity provision. Annual emissions started at 10 million VVV per year at token launch, reduced to 8 million, then to 6 million in February 2026. You can view the current supply [here](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/venice-token/).

Fundamental demand comes from utility. You can stake VVV to earn yield and, when you want tokenized compute, lock your staked VVV (sVVV) to mint DIEM. Staked DIEM provides 1 dollar per day of Venice API credit per token, delivering private, uncensored inference without per-request fees. For example, staking 100 DIEM provides 100 dollars per day of API credit.

questions.title: Is the supply capped for the Venice token (VVV)?
questions.markdown: Genesis supply was 100M VVV. On March 12, 2025, Venice executed two token burns totaling 33,539,739 VVV ([view transaction](https://basescan.org/tx/0xf5a19b79b13274faf19c46b404a36699990cad18e907aece0ab26671ff4a37af)). Annual emissions started at 10M VVV per year at token launch, reduced to 8M, then to 6M in February 2026. You can view the current supply [here](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/venice-token/).

questions.title: How does the Venice token (VVV) work and what are the benefits of staking it?
questions.markdown: You can stake VVV to earn emissions. You can also lock your staked VVV (sVVV) to mint **DIEM**, a tokenized compute unit equal to $1 per day of Venice API credit. You may stake **DIEM** for ongoing credit or send, sell, and transfer it, which lets you separate and monetize compute without selling your VVV principal. This expands VVV's utility from individual access to marketable capacity through **DIEM**.

questions.title: How does VVV staking yield work?
questions.markdown: After the **DIEM** upgrade the Utilization Rate split is removed. VVV stakers receive 100 percent of emissions as yield, paid in VVV. If your sVVV is locked to back minted **DIEM** you earn 80 percent of the standard staking yield while locked. Annual emissions are 10 million VVV effective August 20, 2025. At VVV launch emissions were 14 million per year and the Utilization Rate split emissions between stakers and Venice from 20 percent to 80 percent depending on demand.

questions.title: Does staking the Venice Token (VVV) grant access to Pro Features?
questions.markdown: Access to Pro Features is available to users who stake 100 or more Venice Tokens (VVV). Currently, users will still temporarily receive a staking yield on the staked 100 VVV. At a future date, this yield may be revoked, and Venice will give at least fifteen (15) days' notice. [Venice.ai](https://venice.ai) reserves the right to discontinue this staking benefit at any time, at its discretion.

questions.title: When are staking rewards paid out?
questions.markdown: Staking rewards accumulate continually and you can withdraw them whenever you wish.

questions.title: Do my staking rewards expire?
questions.markdown: No, staking rewards for $VVV do not expire. When claiming your rewards, you will need a small amount of $ETH on Base to cover gas fees.

questions.title: How long do my VVV tokens take to unstake?
questions.markdown: When you unstake your VVV, a **7-day cooldown period** begins. The specific amount you've chosen to unstake won't earn rewards during these 7 days. Any remaining VVV still actively staked will continue to accrue rewards normally. After the 7-day period, your unstaked VVV will be available for withdrawal.

questions.title: How do I stake VVV or DIEM?
questions.markdown:
Navigate to [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token) and connect your wallet in the top right. There you will see the options to stake VVV and DIEM.

**IMPORTANT: DO NOT SEND TOKENS DIRECTLY TO THE CONTRACT ADDRESS.**

Tokens sent to a contract address cannot be recovered. Always use the staking page.

questions.title: How do I mint DIEM?
questions.markdown: To mint **DIEM** you need to lock sVVV at the current Mint Rate. The Mint Rate determines how much sVVV is required to mint 1 **DIEM** and increases as supply grows. After minting, **DIEM** appears in your wallet as an ERC-20 token. You can mint on the Venice token dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: How do I use DIEM for inference?
questions.markdown:
Stake **DIEM** to receive $1 per day of API credit per token. This credit is perpetual and stable. You can stake **DIEM** on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

You'll see your **DIEM** allocation on the API page at [venice.ai/settings/api](https://venice.ai/settings/api).

questions.title: Is DIEM a stablecoin?
questions.markdown: No. **DIEM** is not pegged to USD. Its market price may move, but each **DIEM** always provides $1 per day of Venice API credit when staked.

questions.title: Can I transfer or trade DIEM?
questions.markdown: Yes. **DIEM** is an ERC-20 token on Base. You can send it to other wallets and trade it on supported platforms.

questions.title: Is there a cooldown to unstake DIEM?
questions.markdown: DIEM can be unstaked after a 1 day cooldown. To unlock sVVV, burn the same amount of DIEM you minted. Any unlocked sVVV can then be unstaked with a 7 day cooldown.

questions.title: How do I unlock my sVVV after minting DIEM?
questions.markdown: Burn the same amount of **DIEM** you minted to unlock **sVVV**. Partial burns are allowed. Once unlocked, sVVV can be unstaked with a 7 day cooldown. You can burn on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: What happens if I sell the DIEM I minted and later want to unlock my sVVV?
questions.markdown: To unlock sVVV you must burn the same amount of **DIEM** you minted. If you sold that **DIEM** you will need to re-acquire it before you can unlock. While locked, your VVV continues to earn yield.

questions.title: If part of my sVVV is in the 7-day unstake cooldown, can I still mint DIEM with the rest?
questions.markdown: Yes. The cooldown applies only to the specific amount you chose to unstake. Any remaining sVVV stays staked and can be locked to mint DIEM at the current Mint Rate. You can mint on the dashboard at [venice.ai/token](https://venice.ai/token).

questions.title: Does burning DIEM give me more sVVV?
questions.markdown:
No. Burning `DIEM` only unlocks the `sVVV` that was originally locked when that `DIEM` was minted.

If you buy `DIEM` on the market and burn it, that does **not** create new `sVVV` or add `sVVV` to your balance. Burning only unlocks the already-locked `sVVV` tied to **your original minting position**.

questions.title: Why do my unclaimed rewards disappear when I stake VVV?
questions.markdown: Your rewards aren't lost - staking additional tokens automatically claims your pending rewards. You can verify this in your transaction history on [BaseScan](https://basescan.org/).

questions.title: What yield do I earn while my sVVV is locked to back DIEM?
questions.markdown: While your sVVV is locked to back **DIEM** you earn 80 percent of the standard VVV staking yield. VVV staked without minting **DIEM** earns 100 percent of emissions. Before the **DIEM** upgrade, VVV stakers earned between 20 percent and 80 percent of emissions.

questions.title: What’s the difference between web search, web scraping, and X Search?
questions.markdown:
These features bring fresh information into Venice in different ways.

- `Web Search` finds current results from the web and helps Venice answer questions with up-to-date information.
- `Web Scraping` pulls content from specific URLs and adds that content to context.
- `X Search` is available on supported Grok models and can search both the web and X in real time.

Use `Web Search` when you want general current information. Use `Web Scraping` when you want Venice to read a specific page. Use `X Search` when you want supported Grok models to use xAI’s native real-time search.

For more details, see the [Web Search docs](https://docs.venice.ai/api-reference/endpoint/augment/search) and [API Pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing).

questions.title: What are the web scraping limits in the Venice UI?
questions.markdown:
When Web Scraping is enabled and your prompt includes URLs, Venice applies a daily scrape limit based on your plan:

- `Anonymous`: 0 per day
- `FREE`: 15 per day
- `PRO`: 100 per day
- `PLUS`: 400 per day
- `MAX`: 1,000 per day

questions.title: How do I generate the same person/face/subject in an image?
questions.markdown:
You can create a consistent person, face, or subject in an image by using image editing or seed values.

One option is to use image editing to modify an existing image while keeping the same subject. Another option is to use seed values so you can generate variations while preserving a similar look.

To use seed values: 1. Create a detailed prompt describing the subject you want. 2. Generate your image. 3. When you find an image you like, open `Image Details`. 4. Copy the seed value. 5. Enter that seed in your image settings. 6. Adjust your prompt while keeping the same seed to create variations of the same subject.

questions.title: How do I make a video from a reference image?
questions.markdown:
To make a video from a reference image, upload the image, choose a video model that supports reference-to-video, and describe the motion, scene, or transformation you want.

This is useful when you want the video to keep a consistent subject, character, or visual identity.

Video generations consume credits. For current model availability and video details, see the [video model docs](https://docs.venice.ai/models/video).

questions.title: Can I upload images into Venice?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Venice supports image uploads for chat, image editing, and other multimodal use cases.

Not all models support vision or image analysis. If you upload an image for analysis while using a model that does not support vision, Venice will swap to a model that does.

You can upload an image when you want Venice to analyze it, use it as the basis for an edit, or use it in a generation workflow.

questions.title: How do I disable the Mature Filter?
questions.markdown:
You can disable the Mature Filter if you have a `Pro` plan or higher.

Open `Settings` and turn the Mature Filter off. You can also access this setting from the model selector.

questions.title: How do I remove the Venice watermark on images?
questions.markdown:
You can remove the Venice watermark on images if you have a `Pro` plan or higher.

Open `Settings`, go to image settings, and turn the watermark option off. You can also access this setting from the prompt bar while using an image model.

questions.title: How do I upload a file for analysis?
questions.markdown:
To upload a file for analysis, use the `+` button to open the action menu and attach your file.

Once the file is uploaded, enter your prompt telling Venice what you want to do with it, such as summarize it, answer questions about it, or analyze specific sections.

All models support document upload.

questions.title: How large of a document can Venice analyze?
questions.markdown:
Venice supports documents up to `10MB` and approximately `250,000` characters.

If you receive an error after uploading a document, the file likely exceeds the current size or length limit.

questions.title: How do I edit an image on Venice?
questions.markdown:
You can edit an image on Venice in three ways.

The first is to use the `+` button to open the action menu and select `Edit Image`, then upload the image you want to change and describe the edit you want in plain language.

You can also edit an image while in `Auto` mode by attaching an image with the `+` button and prompting Venice to make the change you want.

A third option is to use `Venice Studio`, available in the left sidebar, where you can work with supported image editing workflows directly.

Image editing can be used to replace objects, change backgrounds, adjust style, or make more targeted changes to an existing image. Image edits consume credits or image requests depending on the model and workflow you are using.

questions.title: How do I access the API?
questions.markdown:
You can access the Venice API in two ways.

The standard way is to create an API key in your [API Dashboard](https://venice.ai/settings/api). Once you have an API key, you can use the API with your available `USD credits` or `DIEM`.

Venice also supports [\`x402\`](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api), which is an alternative way to access paid API routes using wallet authentication instead of an API key.

For setup details and pricing, see the [API pricing docs](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing) and the [\`x402\` guide](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/x402-venice-api).

questions.title: Where can I see my API usage and credit balances?
questions.markdown:
You can view your API usage, credit spend, and balance details in your [API Dashboard](https://venice.ai/settings/api).

This page shows your API activity and separates your available balance by `DIEM` and `USD`, so you can see how your credits are split.

If you’re trying to understand where your API credits went, this is the best place to check first.

questions.title: Where can I find the API documentation?
questions.markdown:
You can find the Venice API documentation at [docs.venice.ai](https://docs.venice.ai).

For API setup, endpoints, pricing, and rate limits, start with the [API overview](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/about-venice).

questions.title: Is API usage included in my Pro, Pro+, or Max plan?
questions.markdown:
No. Plan perks like unlimited text prompts and daily image limits refer to use inside the Venice web app and mobile app, not the API.

API usage is metered separately. Every API call consumes credits (or charges your balance) based on the model's per-token price, regardless of whether you're on Pro, Pro+, or Max. Paid plans do not include unlimited API usage.

If you use a text model via the API and see your balance going down, that is expected. Even for models that are free inside the web app, API calls are billed per token.

You can find the per-model API rates at [docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing#api-pricing).

questions.title: How do I install Venice on my phone or tablet?
questions.markdown:
Venice is currently available on `Android`.

You can download the app here: [venice.ai/lp/download](https://venice.ai/lp/download). From there, you can install via Google Play or download the Android APK directly.

The **iOS app is temporarily unavailable on the App Store**. We're working to bring it back as soon as possible. If you're on iPhone, you can keep using Venice in the meantime at [venice.ai](https://venice.ai) right from your mobile browser.

questions.title: Can I export an encrypted backup on my mobile device?
questions.markdown:
No. Encrypted Backups are currently managed from the Venice web app, not the iOS or Android mobile apps.

On the web app, Pro users can create an encrypted backup from the chat history drawer by selecting their profile and choosing `Backup Chat History`.

If you need to back up or restore chat history, use Venice in a web browser on a logged-in device.

questions.title: Is there a limit to how much I can use Venice?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Your limits depend on your plan and the feature you’re using.

Free includes 10 text prompts per day and 15 image prompts per day. Pro includes unlimited text prompts, 1,000 image prompts per day, and 100 monthly credits for premium features. Pro+ includes higher image limits plus 7,500 monthly credits, and Max includes the highest image limits plus 22,500 monthly credits.

Premium features such as video, music, frontier models, and API usage consume credits. For the latest limits and plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: What are Venice Credits?
questions.markdown:
Venice Credits are used to access premium features on the platform, including video generation, music, premium models, and API usage.

Every paid plan includes monthly credits. Pro includes 100 per month, Pro+ includes 7,500 per month, and Max includes 22,500 per month. Pro+ includes 2-month credit banking, and Max includes 3-month credit banking.

For the latest plan details and included credits, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing) and [Introducing Venice Pro+ and Max](https://venice.ai/blog/new-subscription-tiers).

questions.title: When do Venice credits reset?
questions.markdown:
Venice credits do not all reset on the same schedule.

Paid plan credits refresh monthly. Pro includes 100 credits per month, Pro+ includes 7,500 per month, and Max includes 22,500 per month.

DIEM-backed credits are different. Credits from staked DIEM refresh daily at `12:01 AM UTC`.

USD credits are also different from DIEM-backed credits. USD credits do not reset daily, and they are not the same as DIEM-backed daily credits.

If you are checking your balance, make sure you know which kind of credits you are using: monthly plan credits, DIEM-backed daily credits, or USD credits. For current plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: Is there a limit to Voice Mode usage?
questions.markdown:
Yes. Voice Mode in the Venice web and mobile apps is limited by a rolling quota of voice audio seconds per 30-day period. The allowance depends on your plan.

- **Pro: **`3,600 seconds` (1 hour per 30 days)
- **Pro+: **`18,000 seconds` (5 hours per 30 days)
- **Max: **`72,000 seconds` (20 hours per 30 days)

These limits apply to Voice Mode usage in the app; they are separate from unlimited text prompts and other plan allowances.

questions.title: What is the refund policy?
questions.markdown:
Venice subscriptions are generally non-refundable once payment has been processed. If you do not want your subscription to renew, you can cancel it before the next billing cycle and your access will remain active through the end of your current paid period.

To manage or cancel your subscription, visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription) or see [How do I cancel my subscription?](https://venice.ai/faqs#how-do-i-cancel-my-subscription).

questions.title: How does the referral program work?
questions.markdown:
When someone signs up using your referral link and upgrades to `Pro`, both of you receive `$10` in API credits.

A referral only qualifies after the referred user completes the required upgrade. You can track referrals from the referral section in Venice.

If you want more details about current plans and credits, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: Why didn’t I get my referral credits?
questions.markdown:
Referral credits are only issued after the referred user completes a qualifying `Pro` upgrade.

If your credits have not appeared yet, check that: - the new user signed up through your referral link - they completed the qualifying upgrade - the referral appears in your referral dashboard - the payment was not refunded or reversed

If everything looks correct and the credits still have not appeared, contact [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

questions.title: Can I pay with crypto?
questions.markdown:
Venice does not broadly accept crypto for all purchases.

Credits can be purchased with `USD` or `USDC`. For subscriptions, `BTC` is accepted through `BTCPay` for yearly plans.

Because payment options depend on what you are buying, always use the payment methods shown in the relevant checkout or billing flow. For current plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing).

questions.title: What are Pro+ and Max?
questions.markdown:
`Pro+` and `Max` are higher-tier Venice plans for users who need more credits and higher usage limits than `Pro`.

`Pro+` includes everything in `Pro`, plus higher image generation limits, `7,500` monthly credits, and `2-month` credit banking. `Max` includes everything in `Pro+`, plus the highest image generation limits, `22,500` monthly credits, and `3-month` credit banking.

If you need more credits for video, music, premium models, or API usage, these plans are designed for heavier use. For the latest plan details, see the [pricing page](https://venice.ai/pricing) and [Introducing Venice Pro+ and Max](https://venice.ai/blog/new-subscription-tiers).

questions.title: How do I cancel my subscription?
questions.markdown:
You can cancel your subscription at any time from your subscription settings.

To manage or cancel your subscription, visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription). If you cancel, your access will remain active until the end of your current billing period.

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription, payment methods, or get receipts?
questions.markdown:
You can manage your subscription, payment methods, billing details, and receipts from your subscription settings.

Visit [venice.ai/subscription](https://venice.ai/subscription) to view or update your billing information.

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription if I purchased through Apple?
questions.markdown:
If you subscribed through the Apple App Store, your subscription is managed directly by Apple.

To manage or cancel it, open the `Settings` app on your iPhone or iPad, tap your name, select `Subscriptions`, and choose `Venice.ai`.

You can also manage Apple subscriptions at [apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions](https://apps.apple.com/account/subscriptions).

questions.title: How do I manage my subscription if I purchased through Google Play?
questions.markdown:
If you subscribed through Google Play, your subscription is managed directly by Google.

To manage or cancel it, open the Google Play Store, tap your profile icon, select `Payments & subscriptions`, then `Subscriptions`, and choose `Venice.ai`.

You can also manage Google Play subscriptions at [play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions](https://play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions).

questions.title: Why have I hit my image limit early?
questions.markdown:
Features like `Enhance`, `Upscale`, and `Multi-Image Edit` use more image requests per use than a standard image generation.

For example: - `Enhance 4x` can use up to 16 image requests - `Multi-Image Edit` uses 4 image requests instead of 2

These image request limits apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How does the Enhance feature consume image requests?
questions.markdown:
The `Enhance` feature uses image requests based on the output size. `Enhance 4x` can use up to 16 image requests per use.

These image request rules apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How do Venice image requests work?
questions.markdown:
Venice gives you a set number of image requests for `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Different actions use those requests at different rates.

- `Generating a single image`: 1 request
- `Editing an image`: 2 requests
- `Multi-image editing`: 4 requests
- `Generating 2, 3, or 4 image variants`: 1 request per image
- `2x Upscale (with enhance)`: up to 8 requests
- `4x Upscale (with enhance)`: up to 16 requests
- `2x Upscale (without enhance)`: up to 10 requests
- `4x Upscale (without enhance)`: up to 20 requests

You can mix and match these actions until all the image requests included in your subscription plan are used up. These limits reset based on your first request in each rate-limit period.

Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: How does the Edit Image feature consume image requests?
questions.markdown:
The `Edit Image` feature uses 2 image requests per edit, regardless of the size or complexity of the edit.

`Multi-image editing` uses 4 image requests per operation.

These image request rules apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: Where can I find how many images I have left for the day?
questions.markdown:
To see how many image requests you have left, open the chat history drawer, go to `Settings`, then open `Storage and Limits`.

Your remaining text and image limits are shown there.

These limits apply to `Free` and `Pro` model usage. Pay-per-use models are separate and are not limited by the image requests included in your subscription plan.

questions.title: Is API usage included in my Pro, Pro+, or Max plan?
questions.markdown:
No. Plan perks like unlimited text prompts and daily image limits refer to use inside the Venice web app and mobile app, not the API.

API usage is metered separately. Every API call consumes credits (or charges your balance) based on the model's per-token price, regardless of whether you're on Pro, Pro+, or Max. Paid plans do not include unlimited API usage.

If you use a text model via the API and see your balance going down, that is expected. Even for models that are free inside the web app, API calls are billed per token.

You can find the per-model API rates at [docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing](https://docs.venice.ai/overview/pricing#api-pricing).

questions.title: How do I delete my account?
questions.markdown:
You can delete your account from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and choose `Delete Account`.

Once an account is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

questions.title: How do I change my account password?
questions.markdown:
You can change your password from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and choose `Update Password`.

questions.title: How do I change my account email?
questions.markdown:
To change your account email, first add the new email address to your account, then set it as your primary email.

You can do this from your account settings by opening `Settings`, going to `Profile`, selecting `Manage Account`, and updating your email settings there.

questions.title: How do I add 2FA on my account?
questions.markdown:
You can enable two-factor authentication from your account settings.

Open `Settings`, go to `Profile`, select `Manage Account`, then open the `Security` section and enable two-factor authentication (`2FA`) for your account.

questions.title: What is the official support email?
questions.markdown: The official email for support is [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

questions.title: Where should I report a security vulnerability?
questions.markdown: Please submit security vulnerabilities through the [Venice Bug Bounty Program](/bug-bounty). The program page explains what is in scope, what to include in your report, and how rewards are handled.

questions.title: WalletConnect users: Why do I keep getting logged out of Venice?
questions.markdown:
WalletConnect sessions expire after 7 days, so if you sign in to Venice with WalletConnect, you may need to log in again after that period.

If your wallet supports longer sessions, you may be able to adjust that in your wallet settings. For wallet-specific session questions, contact your wallet provider.

questions.title: Can you delete my data?
questions.markdown:
You can request deletion of the account information Venice stores, such as your email address.

To submit a deletion request, contact [support@venice.ai](mailto:support@venice.ai).

For more information about Venice’s privacy approach, see [venice.ai/privacy](https://venice.ai/privacy).

questions.title: How do I join the official Venice.ai Discord?
questions.markdown:
You can join the official Venice Discord here: [discord.gg/askvenice](https://discord.gg/askvenice).

On Discord, you can get announcements from the team, connect with the community, and follow product updates.
